name: Signet Policy Engine
description: Policy evaluation engine for the Signet personal sovereign agent stack. Every data request is evaluated as Actor + Predicate + Context = Legitimacy, producing a three-way decision: PERMIT, DENY, or ANOMALY. Implements a six-level sensitivity tier hierarchy (Public < Commerce < Financial < Medical < Identity < TrustedAgent), four actor classification methods (explicit assignment, credential-based, domain inference, self-declaration) with discrete confidence levels, deny-overrides policy combining, fail-secure timeout semantics (pipeline timeout = DENY, not error), structured ANOMALY escalation (who/what/why/options — never silently resolved), and a suggest-only pattern tracker with MAC-protected entries. Policies stored as versioned structured JSON in the vault. No algorithm negotiation. All evaluations are domain-bound and time-limited. Write-ahead audit guarantee for all decisions.

types.name: SensitivityTier
types.description: Six-level sensitivity hierarchy with manual Ord implementation. Exhaustive (no #[non_exhaustive]) so new tiers force compile-time review of all match sites. Ordering: Public < Commerce < Financial < Medical < Identity < TrustedAgent.

types.name: ActorId

fields.name: value
fields.description: Opaque actor identifier string. Must be non-empty and at most 256 bytes UTF-8.

validators.error_message: ActorId must be between 1 and 256 bytes

types.description: Newtype wrapper around a string identifier for actors. Prevents stringly-typed confusion with other ID types.

types.name: PredicateId

fields.name: value
fields.description: Opaque predicate identifier string. Must be non-empty and at most 256 bytes UTF-8.

validators.error_message: PredicateId must be between 1 and 256 bytes

types.description: Newtype wrapper around a string identifier for predicates (the 'what' in a data request). Prevents stringly-typed confusion.

types.name: DomainId

fields.name: value
fields.description: Domain identifier string, typically a DNS name or URI origin. Must be non-empty and at most 512 bytes UTF-8.

validators.error_message: DomainId must be between 1 and 512 bytes

types.description: Newtype wrapper around a string identifier for domains. Used for domain-binding validation of proofs and requests.

types.name: PolicyVersion

fields.name: value
fields.description: Monotonically increasing version number, starting from 1.

validators.error_message: PolicyVersion must be a positive integer

types.description: Newtype wrapper for monotonically increasing policy set version numbers. Must be positive.

types.name: Timestamp

fields.name: seconds_since_epoch
fields.description: Seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z UTC.

fields.name: nanoseconds
fields.description: Sub-second nanosecond offset, 0..999_999_999.

validators.error_message: Nanoseconds must be in range 0..999_999_999

types.description: UTC timestamp represented as seconds since Unix epoch plus nanosecond offset. Used for expiration, audit, and time-bounding.

types.name: ConfidenceLevel
types.description: Discrete four-level confidence enum for actor classification. No floating point — each level has fixed semantics. Ordering: Low < Medium < High < Verified.

types.name: ClassificationMethod
types.description: How an actor's sensitivity tier was determined. Each variant carries different trust implications. Explicit has highest baseline trust; SelfDeclared has lowest.

types.name: ClassificationEvidence

fields.name: method
fields.description: How this evidence was obtained.

fields.name: tier_claim
fields.description: The sensitivity tier this evidence supports.

fields.name: evidence_ref
fields.description: Opaque reference to the backing evidence (credential hash, domain record, etc.). Must not contain plaintext secrets.

validators.error_message: Evidence reference must be between 1 and 1024 bytes

fields.name: obtained_at
fields.description: When this evidence was obtained.

types.description: A single piece of evidence used to classify an actor. Combines the method of classification with supporting data.

types.name: ActorClassification

fields.name: actor_id
fields.description: The actor that was classified.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: The determined sensitivity tier for this actor.

fields.name: method
fields.description: The primary classification method that determined the tier.

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Discrete confidence level in the classification.

fields.name: evidence_count
fields.description: Number of evidence items considered.

validators.error_message: Evidence count must be non-negative and bounded

fields.name: classified_at
fields.description: When this classification was performed.

types.description: The result of classifying an actor: their determined sensitivity tier, the method used, confidence level, and all evidence considered.

types.name: RequestContext

fields.name: domain_id
fields.description: The domain this request is bound to. Used for domain-binding validation.

fields.name: request_timestamp
fields.description: When the request was made. Used for time-limiting and freshness checks.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata pairs. Keys and values are strings. Must not contain secret material.

types.description: Contextual information accompanying an evaluation request: domain binding, timestamp, and optional metadata key-value pairs.

types.name: EvaluationRequest

fields.name: actor_id
fields.description: The actor requesting data access.

fields.name: predicate_id
fields.description: What data or action is being requested.

fields.name: actor_classification
fields.description: Pre-computed classification of the actor. Must have been classified before evaluation.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Domain, timestamp, and metadata context for this request.

types.description: A complete policy evaluation request: who (actor) wants what (predicate) in what context. This is the input to PolicyEvaluator::evaluate.

types.name: DenyReason
types.description: Structured reason for a DENY decision. Timeout variant implements fail-secure semantics. InsufficientTier indicates the actor's tier is below the predicate's minimum. PolicyRuleDeny indicates an explicit deny rule matched. Revoked indicates the actor's access was revoked. ExpiredContext indicates the request context timestamp is stale.

types.name: Provenance

fields.name: policy_version
fields.description: Version of the PolicySet used for this evaluation.

fields.name: matching_rule_ids
fields.description: Identifiers of the policy rules that contributed to this PERMIT decision.

fields.name: evaluated_at
fields.description: Timestamp when the evaluation was performed.

types.description: Traceability record linking a PERMIT decision back to the specific policy rules and version that authorized it.

types.name: AnomalyReport

fields.name: who
fields.description: The actor whose request triggered the anomaly.

fields.name: what
fields.description: The predicate (data/action) that was requested.

fields.name: why_unusual
fields.description: Human-readable structured reasoning explaining why this request is anomalous.

validators.error_message: Anomaly reason must be between 1 and 4096 bytes

fields.name: options
fields.description: Available resolution options presented to the user. Must contain at least one option.

fields.name: anomaly_factors
fields.description: Machine-readable list of factors that contributed to the anomaly determination.

fields.name: request_context
fields.description: The original request context for audit trail.

fields.name: detected_at
fields.description: When the anomaly was detected.

types.description: Structured escalation payload for ANOMALY decisions. Contains who, what, why unusual, and available options. Must never be silently resolved — requires explicit user action.

types.name: AnomalyOption

fields.name: option_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this option within the anomaly report.

validators.error_message: Option ID must be between 1 and 128 bytes

fields.name: label
fields.description: Human-readable label for the option.

validators.error_message: Option label must be between 1 and 256 bytes

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed description of what this option does.

validators.error_message: Option description must be between 1 and 1024 bytes

fields.name: resulting_decision
fields.description: The decision type this option would produce: 'permit' or 'deny'.

validators.error_message: Resulting decision must be 'permit' or 'deny'

types.description: A single resolution option for an ANOMALY decision. Presented to the user for explicit action.

types.name: Decision
types.description: Three-way evaluation output. Permit carries expiration and provenance. Deny carries structured reason (including Timeout for fail-secure). Anomaly carries full escalation report. Anomaly is never silently resolved.

types.name: PermitDecision

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: When this permit expires. All permits are time-limited.

fields.name: provenance
fields.description: Traceability back to the policy rules that authorized this decision.

types.description: Payload for a PERMIT decision. Includes expiration timestamp and provenance for audit traceability.

types.name: DenyDecision

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Structured reason for denial.

fields.name: policy_version
fields.description: Version of the PolicySet that produced this denial.

types.description: Payload for a DENY decision. Carries structured reason. DenyReason::Timeout is used for fail-secure pipeline timeout.

types.name: AnomalyDecision

fields.name: report
fields.description: Complete structured anomaly report with who/what/why/options.

types.description: Payload for an ANOMALY decision. Carries full structured escalation report. Must trigger escalation flow.

types.name: PolicyRuleKind
types.description: Discriminator for policy rule types. Used as serde internally-tagged enum via #[serde(tag = "type")]. Each variant corresponds to a different rule structure.

types.name: PolicyRule

fields.name: rule_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this rule within the PolicySet.

validators.error_message: Rule ID must be between 1 and 128 bytes

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The type of this rule, used as serde tag discriminator.

fields.name: actor_pattern
fields.description: Actor matching pattern. '*' for all actors, or a specific ActorId value, or a tier-level pattern like 'tier:Commerce+'.

validators.error_message: Actor pattern must be between 1 and 512 bytes

fields.name: predicate_pattern
fields.description: Predicate matching pattern. '*' for all predicates, or a specific PredicateId value, or a category pattern.

validators.error_message: Predicate pattern must be between 1 and 512 bytes

fields.name: minimum_tier
fields.description: Minimum sensitivity tier required for this rule to apply (for TierThreshold rules) or the tier context for other rule kinds.

fields.name: domain_constraint
fields.description: Optional domain constraint. If set, rule only applies to requests from this domain.

fields.name: valid_from
fields.description: Start of the time window this rule is valid. For TimeWindow rules this is required; for others it defaults to epoch.

fields.name: valid_until
fields.description: End of the time window this rule is valid. For TimeWindow rules this is required; for others it defaults to far future.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Rule priority for ordering within same-kind rules. Higher value = higher priority. Does not override deny-overrides combining.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether this rule is active. Disabled rules are skipped during evaluation.

types.description: A single policy rule with serde internally-tagged serialization. Versioned schema. Each rule has an ID, kind, and kind-specific parameters encoded as structured JSON fields.

types.name: PolicySet

fields.name: version
fields.description: Monotonically increasing version number for this policy set.

fields.name: rules
fields.description: Ordered list of policy rules. Evaluated with deny-overrides combining.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When this policy set version was created.

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Schema version for forward compatibility. Current version is 1.

validators.error_message: Only schema version 1 is currently supported

types.description: Versioned collection of policy rules. Serialized as JSON with deny_unknown_fields in strict deserialization mode. Stored in the vault.

types.name: PolicySnapshot

fields.name: policy_set
fields.description: The frozen policy set for this evaluation.

fields.name: snapshot_taken_at
fields.description: When the snapshot was taken. Used for freshness validation.

types.description: An immutable, point-in-time snapshot of a PolicySet used for TOCTOU-safe evaluation. Once taken, the snapshot is used for the entire evaluation pipeline.

types.name: PatternRecord

fields.name: actor_id
fields.description: The actor in this pattern.

fields.name: predicate_id
fields.description: The predicate in this pattern.

fields.name: approve_count
fields.description: Number of times the user approved this actor+predicate pair.

validators.error_message: Approve count must be non-negative

fields.name: deny_count
fields.description: Number of times the user denied this actor+predicate pair.

validators.error_message: Deny count must be non-negative

fields.name: first_seen
fields.description: Timestamp of the first recorded decision for this pair.

fields.name: last_seen
fields.description: Timestamp of the most recent recorded decision for this pair.

fields.name: mac
fields.description: HMAC-SHA-256 over all other fields. Computed using a key derived from the vault. Detects tampering.

types.description: Records user approve/deny decisions for a specific actor+predicate pair. MAC-protected to detect tampering. Used by pattern tracker for suggestion generation.

types.name: PatternDecision
types.description: The user's decision on an evaluated request, recorded by the pattern tracker.

types.name: SuggestionThresholds

fields.name: min_occurrences
fields.description: Minimum total decisions (approve + deny) before suggesting a rule.

validators.error_message: Minimum occurrences must be between 1 and 10000

fields.name: approval_ratio_permit
fields.description: Minimum approval percentage (0-100) to suggest an auto-permit rule.

validators.error_message: Approval ratio must be between 0 and 100

fields.name: denial_ratio_deny
fields.description: Minimum denial percentage (0-100) to suggest an auto-deny rule.

validators.error_message: Denial ratio must be between 0 and 100

types.description: Configuration for when the pattern tracker should generate suggestions. Simple counter/threshold, not ML.

types.name: PolicySuggestion

fields.name: suggested_rule
fields.description: The policy rule being suggested.

fields.name: based_on_record
fields.description: The pattern record that triggered this suggestion.

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Human-readable explanation of why this rule is suggested.

validators.error_message: Rationale must be between 1 and 2048 bytes

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Confidence level in this suggestion based on pattern strength.

types.description: A suggested policy rule generated by the pattern tracker based on observed user decisions. Suggest-only, never auto-applied.

types.name: AuditEventKind
types.description: Types of auditable events emitted by the policy engine.

types.name: AuditEvent

fields.name: event_kind
fields.description: The type of audit event.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: When the event occurred.

fields.name: actor_id
fields.description: The actor involved, if applicable.

fields.name: predicate_id
fields.description: The predicate involved, if applicable.

fields.name: domain_id
fields.description: The domain context, if applicable.

fields.name: decision_summary
fields.description: Brief summary of the decision or action. Must not contain sensitive data.

fields.name: policy_version
fields.description: The policy version in effect, if applicable.

types.description: A structured audit event emitted to the audit chain. All policy decisions and significant actions produce audit events.

types.name: TimeoutConfig

fields.name: evaluation_timeout_ms
fields.description: Maximum time in milliseconds for a complete evaluation pipeline. On expiry, returns Deny::Timeout.

validators.error_message: Evaluation timeout must be between 1ms and 60000ms

fields.name: classification_timeout_ms
fields.description: Maximum time in milliseconds for actor classification. On expiry, classification fails and evaluation returns Deny::Timeout.

validators.error_message: Classification timeout must be between 1ms and 30000ms

types.description: Configuration for the fail-secure evaluation timeout. When the timeout expires, the pipeline returns Decision::Deny with DenyReason::Timeout.

types.name: PolicyError
types.description: Single error enum for all policy engine operations. Implemented via thiserror. Display impls must never contain sensitive data (no key material, no raw evidence, no plaintext). Crypto failures return generic variants (no oracle). Timeouts produce Decision::Deny, not PolicyError.

types.name: PolicyErrorDetail

fields.name: variant
fields.description: Which PolicyError variant this is.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message. Must not contain sensitive data.

validators.error_message: Error message must be between 1 and 1024 bytes

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling.

types.description: Structured error detail accompanying a PolicyError variant. Contains a safe message and an optional error code, but never sensitive data.

functions.name: evaluate
functions.description: Core policy evaluation: Actor + Predicate + Context = Decision. Takes a PolicySnapshot for TOCTOU safety and an EvaluationRequest, applies deny-overrides combining over all matching rules, and returns a three-way Decision (Permit/Deny/Anomaly). If the evaluation pipeline exceeds the configured timeout, returns Decision::Deny with DenyReason::Timeout — not an error. Emits an audit event for every evaluation via AuditSink (write-ahead guarantee).

inputs.name: snapshot
inputs.description: Immutable point-in-time policy snapshot. Provides TOCTOU safety.

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: The evaluation request containing actor, predicate, classification, and context.

inputs.name: timeout_config
inputs.description: Timeout configuration for fail-secure semantics.

error_cases.name: invalid_request
error_cases.condition: EvaluationRequest fails validation (empty actor_id, empty predicate_id, mismatched classification)

error_cases.name: snapshot_stale
error_cases.condition: PolicySnapshot is older than the configured maximum snapshot age

error_cases.name: audit_sink_failure
error_cases.condition: AuditSink.emit fails and write-ahead guarantee cannot be met — evaluation aborts

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected internal error during rule matching or combining

preconditions: request.actor_classification.actor_id == request.actor_id (classification must match the requesting actor)
request.context.request_timestamp is not in the future (clock skew tolerance: 30 seconds)
request.context.domain_id is non-empty and syntactically valid
snapshot.policy_set.rules is not empty (at least one rule must exist)
request.actor_classification.classified_at is not stale (within configured freshness window)
postconditions: Return value is exactly one of Permit, Deny, or Anomaly — never a compound
If any matching rule is a Deny rule, the result is Deny (deny-overrides combining)
If the timeout elapsed, the result is Deny with reason Timeout regardless of partial evaluation state
An AuditEvent with kind EvaluationCompleted is emitted before the Decision is returned (write-ahead)
If result is Permit, PermitDecision.expires_at > current time
If result is Permit, PermitDecision.provenance.policy_version == snapshot.policy_set.version
If result is Anomaly, AnomalyReport.options is non-empty (at least one resolution option)
If result is Anomaly, an AuditEvent with kind AnomalyEscalated is also emitted
side_effects: Emits AuditEvent(EvaluationCompleted) via AuditSink (write-ahead, before returning)
Emits AuditEvent(AnomalyEscalated) via AuditSink if decision is Anomaly

structured_side_effects.description: Write-ahead audit event emission for every evaluation
Structured trace log of rule matching (no sensitive data)

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of rules in PolicySet

functions.name: classify
functions.description: Classify an actor's sensitivity tier based on provided evidence. Examines all ClassificationEvidence items, applies confidence-weighted tier determination, and returns an ActorClassification. Uses the highest-confidence evidence; in case of tie, uses the most restrictive (lowest) tier. Emits a ClassificationPerformed audit event.

inputs.name: actor_id
inputs.description: The actor to classify.

inputs.name: evidence
inputs.description: All available classification evidence for this actor. Must contain at least one item.

error_cases.name: no_evidence
error_cases.condition: Evidence list is empty

error_cases.name: invalid_evidence
error_cases.condition: One or more evidence items fail validation (empty evidence_ref, future timestamp)

error_cases.name: audit_sink_failure
error_cases.condition: AuditSink.emit fails for the ClassificationPerformed event

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected internal error during classification logic

preconditions: evidence list is non-empty (at least one ClassificationEvidence item)
All evidence items have valid, non-future obtained_at timestamps
No evidence item contains plaintext secrets in evidence_ref
postconditions: Returned ActorClassification.actor_id == input actor_id
Returned ActorClassification.tier is one of the tiers claimed in the evidence
Returned ActorClassification.confidence reflects the strength of the best evidence
Returned ActorClassification.evidence_count == length of input evidence list
Returned ActorClassification.classified_at is approximately current time
An AuditEvent with kind ClassificationPerformed is emitted
If multiple evidence items have the same confidence, the most restrictive (lowest) tier is selected

structured_side_effects.description: Structured trace of evidence evaluation (no sensitive data)

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of evidence items

functions.name: record_pattern
functions.description: Record a user's approve/deny decision for an actor+predicate pair into the pattern tracker. Creates a new PatternRecord if none exists, or updates an existing one. Computes HMAC-SHA-256 MAC over all record fields using a vault-derived key. Emits a PatternRecorded audit event.

inputs.name: actor_id
inputs.description: The actor in the decision being recorded.

inputs.name: predicate_id
inputs.description: The predicate in the decision being recorded.

inputs.name: decision
inputs.description: Whether the user approved or denied the request.

inputs.name: decided_at
inputs.description: When the user made the decision.

inputs.name: mac_key
inputs.description: HMAC-SHA-256 key derived from the vault, used to MAC-protect the pattern record. Must be exactly 32 bytes.

error_cases.name: invalid_mac_key
error_cases.condition: mac_key is not exactly 32 bytes

error_cases.name: mac_verification_failed
error_cases.condition: Existing PatternRecord MAC verification fails (tampered record detected)

error_cases.name: audit_sink_failure
error_cases.condition: AuditSink.emit fails for the PatternRecorded event

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected internal error during record update

preconditions: mac_key is exactly 32 bytes
decided_at is not in the future (clock skew tolerance: 30 seconds)
actor_id and predicate_id are non-empty
postconditions: Returned PatternRecord.actor_id == input actor_id
Returned PatternRecord.predicate_id == input predicate_id
If decision is Approved, returned PatternRecord.approve_count is incremented by 1 from previous value (or 1 if new)
If decision is Denied, returned PatternRecord.deny_count is incremented by 1 from previous value (or 1 if new)
Returned PatternRecord.last_seen == decided_at
Returned PatternRecord.first_seen <= decided_at
Returned PatternRecord.mac is a valid HMAC-SHA-256 over all other fields using mac_key
An AuditEvent with kind PatternRecorded is emitted
mac_key is zeroized from memory after MAC computation
side_effects: Mutates pattern tracker state (creates or updates PatternRecord)
Zeroizes mac_key from memory after use

functions.name: suggest_rules
functions.description: Analyze all pattern records against the provided thresholds and generate policy rule suggestions. Returns suggestions for any actor+predicate pairs that exceed the configured occurrence and ratio thresholds. Suggestions are suggest-only and never auto-applied. Verifies MAC on each PatternRecord before considering it. Emits a SuggestionGenerated audit event for each suggestion produced.

inputs.name: thresholds
inputs.description: Threshold configuration for when to generate suggestions.

inputs.name: mac_key
inputs.description: HMAC-SHA-256 key for verifying PatternRecord MACs. Must be exactly 32 bytes.

error_cases.name: invalid_mac_key
error_cases.condition: mac_key is not exactly 32 bytes

error_cases.name: audit_sink_failure
error_cases.condition: AuditSink.emit fails for a SuggestionGenerated event

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected internal error during suggestion generation

preconditions: mac_key is exactly 32 bytes
thresholds.min_occurrences >= 1
thresholds.approval_ratio_permit is between 0 and 100
thresholds.denial_ratio_deny is between 0 and 100
postconditions: Every returned PolicySuggestion has confidence level proportional to the pattern strength
Every returned PolicySuggestion.based_on_record has a valid MAC
No suggestions are auto-applied to any PolicySet
An AuditEvent with kind SuggestionGenerated is emitted for each suggestion
PatternRecords with invalid MACs are skipped (not included in suggestions) and logged
mac_key is zeroized from memory after all MAC verifications
Returned list may be empty if no patterns exceed thresholds
side_effects: Reads all PatternRecords from PatternTracker storage
Emits AuditEvent(SuggestionGenerated) for each suggestion via AuditSink
Zeroizes mac_key from memory after use

structured_side_effects.description: Logs skipped records with invalid MACs (no sensitive data)

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of PatternRecords

functions.name: load_policies
functions.description: Load the current PolicySet from vault storage. Deserializes from JSON with deny_unknown_fields in strict mode. Validates schema version, rule integrity, and version monotonicity. Returns the PolicySet or an error.

inputs.name: policy_data
inputs.description: Raw JSON bytes of the serialized PolicySet, as retrieved from the vault.

error_cases.name: deserialization_error
error_cases.condition: JSON is malformed, missing required fields, or contains unknown fields (strict mode)

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: PolicySet passes JSON parsing but fails semantic validation (duplicate rule IDs, invalid schema version, invalid tier references)

error_cases.name: load_error
error_cases.condition: Input data is empty, not valid UTF-8, or exceeds 10MB size limit

preconditions: policy_data is valid UTF-8
policy_data is valid JSON
policy_data length > 0 and <= 10MB
postconditions: Returned PolicySet has a valid PolicyVersion (>= 1)
Returned PolicySet has schema_version == 1
All PolicyRules in the returned set have non-empty rule_id values
All rule_id values within the PolicySet are unique
Deserialization used deny_unknown_fields (strict mode)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure deserialization and validation, no side effects

functions.name: save_policies
functions.description: Serialize a PolicySet to JSON bytes for storage in the vault. Validates the PolicySet before serialization. Emits a PolicySetUpdated audit event. Returns the serialized bytes.

inputs.name: policy_set
inputs.description: The PolicySet to serialize and save.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: PolicySet fails pre-serialization validation (duplicate rule IDs, empty rules, invalid version)

error_cases.name: serialization_error
error_cases.condition: JSON serialization fails (should not occur with valid types, but handled defensively)

error_cases.name: audit_sink_failure
error_cases.condition: AuditSink.emit fails for the PolicySetUpdated event

preconditions: policy_set.version >= 1
policy_set.schema_version == 1
All rule_id values within policy_set.rules are unique
policy_set.rules contains at least one rule
postconditions: Returned bytes are valid UTF-8 JSON
Returned bytes can be round-tripped through load_policies to produce an equivalent PolicySet
An AuditEvent with kind PolicySetUpdated is emitted
Serialized JSON uses deny_unknown_fields-compatible structure

structured_side_effects.description: Audit event for policy set update

functions.name: take_snapshot
functions.description: Create an immutable PolicySnapshot from the current PolicySet for TOCTOU-safe evaluation. The snapshot freezes the policy state at a point in time and is used for the entire evaluation pipeline.

inputs.name: policy_set
inputs.description: The current PolicySet to snapshot.

inputs.name: snapshot_time
inputs.description: The timestamp to record as the snapshot time. Should be the current time.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: PolicySet fails validation checks

preconditions: policy_set is a valid, fully-loaded PolicySet
snapshot_time is approximately current time (within 30 seconds)
postconditions: Returned PolicySnapshot.policy_set is a deep copy of the input policy_set
Returned PolicySnapshot.snapshot_taken_at == snapshot_time
Modifications to the original policy_set after this call do not affect the returned snapshot

structured_side_effects.description: Pure snapshot creation, no side effects

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of rules (deep copy)

functions.name: resolve_anomaly
functions.description: Record the user's explicit resolution of an ANOMALY decision. Takes the original AnomalyReport and the user's chosen option. Validates the chosen option is one of the presented options. Emits an AnomalyResolved audit event. Returns the final Decision (Permit or Deny) based on the chosen option. ANOMALY decisions are never silently resolved — this function is the only valid resolution path.

inputs.name: report
inputs.description: The original AnomalyReport from the ANOMALY decision.

inputs.name: chosen_option_id
inputs.description: The option_id of the AnomalyOption the user selected.

validators.error_message: Chosen option ID must be between 1 and 128 bytes

inputs.name: snapshot
inputs.description: Current policy snapshot for generating the resulting Decision with proper provenance.

inputs.name: resolved_at
inputs.description: Timestamp of the user's resolution action.

error_cases.name: invalid_option
error_cases.condition: chosen_option_id does not match any option in report.options

error_cases.name: stale_anomaly
error_cases.condition: The anomaly report is older than the configured maximum anomaly age

error_cases.name: audit_sink_failure
error_cases.condition: AuditSink.emit fails for the AnomalyResolved event

error_cases.name: snapshot_stale
error_cases.condition: PolicySnapshot is older than configured maximum age

preconditions: chosen_option_id matches one of the option_ids in report.options
resolved_at is not before report.detected_at (resolution cannot precede detection)
resolved_at is approximately current time (within 30 seconds)
postconditions: Returned Decision is either Permit or Deny, never Anomaly (anomaly is resolved)
If chosen option has resulting_decision 'permit', returns Permit with proper provenance and expiration
If chosen option has resulting_decision 'deny', returns Deny with reason PolicyRuleDeny
An AuditEvent with kind AnomalyResolved is emitted before returning
The audit event includes the chosen_option_id for traceability

structured_side_effects.description: Write-ahead audit event for anomaly resolution

functions.name: emit_audit
functions.description: Emit a structured audit event to the audit chain. This is the AuditSink trait's core operation. All audit events must be durably recorded before the triggering operation returns (write-ahead guarantee). Events must not contain sensitive data.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The structured audit event to emit.

error_cases.name: audit_sink_failure
error_cases.condition: The underlying audit storage is unavailable or the write fails

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: The AuditEvent fails validation (future timestamp, missing required fields for the event kind)

preconditions: event.timestamp is not in the future (clock skew tolerance: 30 seconds)
event.decision_summary does not contain sensitive data (no key material, no raw evidence)
postconditions: If returns true, the event is durably recorded on the audit chain
Event ordering is preserved (events emitted in sequence are recorded in sequence)
The event is immutable once recorded
side_effects: Durably records the audit event to the audit chain

functions.name: validate_policy_set
functions.description: Perform comprehensive validation of a PolicySet without loading or saving it. Checks structural integrity, rule ID uniqueness, tier consistency, pattern validity, domain constraint syntax, time window coherence, and schema version compatibility. Pure function with no side effects.

inputs.name: policy_set
inputs.description: The PolicySet to validate.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: One or more validation checks fail. Error message describes which checks failed without leaking sensitive data.

postconditions: If returns true, the PolicySet is structurally valid and safe to use in evaluation
If returns true, all rule_id values are unique
If returns true, all time windows have valid_from < valid_until where both are specified
If returns true, schema_version is supported (currently only 1)
If returns true, all SensitivityTier references are valid enum variants

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of rules

functions.name: compare_tiers
functions.description: Compare two SensitivityTier values according to the strict ordering: Public < Commerce < Financial < Medical < Identity < TrustedAgent. Returns -1 if left < right, 0 if equal, 1 if left > right. Pure function, no side effects.

inputs.name: left
inputs.description: The left-hand tier to compare.

inputs.name: right
inputs.description: The right-hand tier to compare.

postconditions: Return value is exactly -1, 0, or 1
compare_tiers(a, b) == -compare_tiers(b, a) (antisymmetry)
compare_tiers(a, a) == 0 (reflexivity)
If compare_tiers(a, b) == -1 and compare_tiers(b, c) == -1 then compare_tiers(a, c) == -1 (transitivity)
compare_tiers(Public, TrustedAgent) == -1

invariants: Vault server never has access to plaintext policy decisions or evaluation context
ANOMALY decisions are never silently resolved — every ANOMALY must be escalated with a complete AnomalyReport and recorded on the audit chain
Pipeline timeout always produces Decision::Deny with reason DenyReason::Timeout, never an error
Deny-overrides combining: any matching DENY rule in the PolicySet wins regardless of other matching rules
SensitivityTier ordering is strictly Public < Commerce < Financial < Medical < Identity < TrustedAgent with no equivalences
No algorithm negotiation in any protocol path — combining algorithm is fixed as deny-overrides
Pattern tracker suggestions are suggest-only and never auto-applied to the active PolicySet
All pattern tracker entries are MAC-protected (HMAC-SHA-256) to detect tampering
One-way data flow: vault -> agent -> service, never reverse — policy decisions flow outward only
All disclosures resulting from PERMIT decisions are recorded on the audit chain via AuditSink
Error Display implementations never contain sensitive data (no key material, no raw actor evidence, no plaintext predicates)
Crypto failures return generic error variants (no oracle information leakage)
PolicySnapshot provides TOCTOU mitigation — evaluation uses a consistent snapshot of policies
No unwrap() or expect() in library code — all paths return Result
PolicyVersion is monotonically increasing within a PolicySet
EvaluationRequest domain_id and timestamp are validated before evaluation begins
All decisions include provenance tracing back to the originating policy rules
requires: serde and serde_json for policy rule serialization (internally-tagged JSON)
time crate for timestamps (preferred over chrono per audit posture)
aws-lc-rs for HMAC-SHA-256 MAC on pattern tracker entries
